name: Faculty of Mining and Minerals Technology (FMMT)
description: The Faculty of Mining and Minerals Technology is the flagship academic unit of UMaT. It is the direct descendant of the original Tarkwa School of Mines and represents the university's core competency. The faculty is bifurcated into extraction (Mining) and processing (Minerals)

name: Faculty of Geosciences and Environmental Studies (FGES)
description: As the extractive industries face increasing scrutiny regarding their ecological footprint, the Faculty of Geosciences and Environmental Studies has grown in strategic importance. This faculty manages the "earth" and "safety" aspects of the curriculum

name: Faculty of Engineering (FoE)
description: The Faculty of Engineering represents UMaT’s diversification beyond the earth sciences. It houses the classical engineering disciplines—Electrical, Mechanical, and Renewable Energy—that are essential for the operation of heavy industry

name: School of Railways and Infrastructure Development (SRID)
description: The School of Railways and Infrastructure Development (SRID) is the most significant strategic expansion in UMaT’s recent history. Located at the Essikado Campus (occupying the old Railway Training School facilities), this school is a direct response to the Government of Ghana’s Master Plan to revitalize the railway network

name: Faculty of Integrated Management Science (FIMS)
description: Engineering excellence without management competence leads to project failure. FIMS was established to breed "Management Engineers."

name: Faculty of Computing and Mathematical Sciences (FCaMS)
description: This faculty provides the mathematical bedrock for all other departments

name: School of Petroleum Studies (SPeTs)
description: Located on the main campus, this school was UMaT’s answer to Ghana’s discovery of oil in commercial quantities in 2007
